title: Disability statistics data package | Stats NZ
content:
## Disability statistics data package
08 September 2017, 10:45am
Download the disability survey data package. The data is sourced from the Disability survey: 2013 and Disability survey: 2006 , which collected information about the characteristics of disabled people, including the nature and cause of impairments, the type of support they need, and how well they are faring compared with non-disabled people.

## About the data package
The geography is for district health board (DHB) areas, with some aggregation of DHBs. The survey results are for the resident population living in private households.
Please consider the information in the sampling error tables and technical notes when analysing data from these tables.
Disability describes an impairment that has a long-term, limiting effect on a person’s ability to carry out day-to-day activities. ‘Long-term’ is defined as six months or longer. ‘Limiting effect’ means a restriction or lack of ability to perform. People were not considered to have a disability if an assistive device (such as glasses or crutches) eliminated their impairment.
Disability survey 2013 data package (CSV zipped, 105 KB)
The file includes the specifications, technical notes, and metadata relevant to Disability survey: 2013 .
Sampling error tables (Excel, 25 KB)
Technical notes (Excel, 38.5 KB)
Table 1 Disability status DHB area, ethnicity and sex for population resident in private households (Excel, 22 KB)
Table 2 Disability status DHB area, age group and sex for population resident in private households (Excel, 29 KB)

- Statistics for university staff and students 13 January 2021
